title: polylepis racemosa subsp. lanata
intro: Polylepis racemosa subsp. lanata, also known as the Queñua or Andean willow, is a species of flowering plant in the family Salicaceae, native to the high-altitude regions of the Andes mountain range. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of polyphenols and flavonoids. The health benefits of Queñua include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Queñua include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made from its leaves, bark, or flowers, and are often used to treat a range of ailments, from colds and flu to digestive issues and skin conditions.
